based_on: Byte-precise mutation of fixtures/mazur.golden.jsonl (engine-emitted canonical bytes). The fixture differs from the golden in exactly two places: the fixture_status block (3 fields) and the single targeted numeric value.

what_was_NOT_mutated: updates[4].weight_before (0.4 — left operand of Rule 6 sum)
updates[4].update (-0.0410835203 — right operand of Rule 6 sum)
updates[4].gradient + updates[4].optimizer.* (Rule 4 + Rule 5 still pass — gradient and update are internally consistent)

parameters_after.w5 (0.358916480 — derived from parameters_before + update; Rule 7 still passes because it uses parameters_before + update, NOT the stored weight_after; if a future Rule 7 implementation also uses stored weight_after, this fixture would need a sibling update)

single_target_invariant: Reconciliation produces exactly one Rule 6 failure naming updates[4].weight_after on parameter w5. Rule 5 must NOT fire (update is unchanged). Rule 7 must NOT fire (parameters_after.w5 is consistent with parameters_before + update, which is the spec form of Rule 7).
failure_priority_assertion: Per docs/reconciliation.md, reconciliation failures report before fixture-lifecycle failures.
purpose: Anti-circularity (Csmith / CompCert lineage): proves the reconciler checks Rule 6 (weight_after == weight_before + update) by detecting a 1e-6 mismatch (1000x the 1e-9 tolerance) between stored weight_after and the sum of (unmutated) weight_before + update. This is the cleanest single-rule fixture in v0.2 — no cascades.
